clusters.title: Trump's Name Removed from Kennedy Center After Court Deadline
clusters.summary: Workers have begun the process of removing Donald Trump's name from the Kennedy Center following a court-ordered deadline that he failed to meet. The removal comes amid ongoing discussions about the appropriateness of honoring political figures in cultural institutions. This action highlights the intersection of art, politics, and public sentiment regarding Trump's legacy.

items.title: Kennedy Center Begins Removing Trump’s Name From Facade
items.description: The arts institution is following a judge’s order to take President Trump’s name off its facade. A 12-hour extension gives it until noon Saturday to complete the work.

items.title: Trump's name being removed from Kennedy Center after judge order
items.description: Crews erected scaffolding on Friday as onlookers gathered into the evening, though storms delayed the work until Saturday.

clusters.title: US Orders Anthropic to Disable Advanced AI Models for Foreign Users
clusters.summary: Anthropic has announced it will disable access to its advanced AI models, Mythos 5 and Fable 5, for all foreign nationals following a directive from the US government. The order was issued due to national security concerns, with officials citing the potential for the technology to be misused. This move reflects ongoing tensions surrounding AI technology and its implications for security and international access.

items.title: Anthropic suspends access to latest AI models following US order - Politico
items.description: Anthropic suspends access to latest AI models following US order  Politico

items.title: Anthropic to disable its most advanced AI models after US order limiting foreign access
items.description:
Company said US government believes safeguards can be bypassed and product used to identify software vulnerabilities

Anthropic said it will “abruptly disable” its most advanced AI models for all users after the US government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, citing national security concerns.

The company received the export control directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all foreign nationals, without being given specific details of the national security concern, Anthropic said in a statement.

Continue reading...

items.title: US orders Anthropic to disable AI models for all foreign nationals
items.description: The company said it received ⁠an export control directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5.

clusters.title: U.S. Military Strike Kills Leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua Gang
clusters.summary: A U.S. military strike has resulted in the death of Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, the alleged leader of the Tren de Aragua gang, with cooperation from the Venezuelan government. The operation, confirmed by President Trump, targets a figure wanted in the United States for criminal activities. This event highlights ongoing U.S. efforts to combat organized crime linked to Venezuela.

items.title: Alleged leader of Tren de Aragua gang killed in U.S. military strike, Trump says
items.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump said that the alleged leader of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua was killed by the U.S. military in cooperation with the Venezuelan government.

items.title: US kills Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua leader in military strike, Trump says - Politico
items.description: US kills Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua leader in military strike, Trump says  Politico

items.title: Trump says leader of Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang killed in US strike
items.description:
President says Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores killed in ‘swift and lethal’ military strike with help from Venezuela

The US military has killed a leader in the Venezuelan street gang Tren de Aragua, Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, with the help of Venezuela, Donald Trump announced on Friday.

“At my direction, the United States Southern Command delivered a swift and lethal kinetic strike to successfully execute Niño Guerrero, the infamous leader of Tren De Aragua, one of the most bloodthirsty Terrorist Organizations on Planet Earth,” Trump wrote on Truth Social.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: SpaceX IPO Launches Musk as World's First Trillionaire
clusters.summary: SpaceX has officially launched its initial public offering (IPO), marking a significant milestone in the space industry and making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. The company's shares began trading on the Nasdaq, reflecting strong investor interest and confidence in its future growth. This event highlights the increasing financial stakes in the commercial space sector and Musk's prominent role within it.

His passing is mourned by many in the creative community, reflecting his lasting impact on contemporary art.

clusters.title: Iran Sets Dates for Funeral and Burial of Supreme Leader Khamenei
clusters.summary: Iran has announced that the funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei will commence on July 4, with burial scheduled for July 9. The ceremonies will take place in Tehran and Qom before he is laid to rest in his hometown of Mashhad. This event marks a significant moment in Iran's political landscape following Khamenei's passing.

clusters.title: Switzerland Votes on Population Cap Proposal of 10 Million
clusters.summary: Swiss voters are set to decide on a controversial proposal to cap the country's population at 10 million, a plan advocated by the right-wing Swiss People's Party as a sustainability measure. Supporters argue it will help manage resources and maintain quality of life, while opponents warn it could lead to economic instability and social unrest. The outcome of the vote could have significant implications for Switzerland's future demographic and economic policies.

clusters.title: Trump Aims for Major Deal with Iran to End Ongoing Conflict
clusters.summary: President Donald Trump is expressing optimism about reaching a significant agreement with Iran aimed at winding down the ongoing conflict. This development comes as Trump prepares for a pivotal weekend that could define his presidency. The potential deal has garnered attention amid ongoing tensions in the region.

clusters.title: Trump Rejects Iran's Account of Deal Terms Amid Drone Attack
clusters.summary: Former President Donald Trump has publicly denied the terms of a leaked deal with Iran, labeling the Iranian account as untrue. He also condemned a recent drone attack, characterizing those responsible as 'dishonorable people.' This exchange highlights ongoing tensions between the U.S. and Iran regarding diplomatic negotiations and security issues in the region.

clusters.title: Appeals Court Confirms Sam Bankman-Fried's Fraud Conviction
clusters.summary: An appeals court has upheld the fraud conviction of Sam Bankman-Fried, the co-founder of the collapsed cryptocurrency exchange FTX. The ruling reinforces the legal consequences of his actions, which have drawn significant attention in the financial and regulatory sectors. This decision marks a pivotal moment in the ongoing scrutiny of cryptocurrency practices and investor protections.

items.title: Appeals court upholds FTX co-founder Sam Bankman-Fried's fraud conviction - CNBC

clusters.title: SpaceX Debuts on Nasdaq with $75 Billion IPO Pricing
clusters.summary: SpaceX has officially debuted on the Nasdaq stock exchange, pricing its initial public offering (IPO) at $135 per share, which values the company at approximately $75 billion. The stock opened at $150, reflecting strong investor interest. This IPO marks a significant milestone for the aerospace company founded by Elon Musk, highlighting the growing demand for space-related ventures.

items.title: SpaceX makes Nasdaq debut at $150 after pricing at $135 - CNBC
items.description: SpaceX makes Nasdaq debut at $150 after pricing at $135  CNBC

items.title: Musk's SpaceX prices record $75 billion IPO at $135 a share - Reuters

clusters.title: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha Passes Away at 47
clusters.summary: Princess Bajrakitiyabha, the second in line to the Thai throne, has died at the age of 47 after spending three years in the hospital due to health complications. Her passing marks a significant moment for the Thai royal family and the nation, as she was a prominent figure in royal duties and public service. The circumstances surrounding her prolonged illness and eventual death have drawn attention and condolences from the public and royal supporters alike.

clusters.title: Melania Trump Launches Savings Account Program for Foster Youth
clusters.summary: Melania Trump has introduced a new savings account initiative aimed at supporting children in foster care. This program, a spinoff of the existing Trump Accounts, is designed to help foster youth build financial stability as they transition to adulthood. The initiative highlights ongoing efforts to address the needs of vulnerable children in the foster care system.

clusters.title: Wholesale Inflation Rises 1.1% in May, Exceeding Expectations
clusters.summary: In May, wholesale inflation increased by 1.1%, surpassing analysts' expectations. This rise was primarily driven by a significant surge in energy prices. The increase in wholesale prices could have implications for consumer costs and economic policy moving forward.

clusters.title: Belfast Protests Intensify Following Stabbing Incident
clusters.summary: Protests erupted in Belfast after a stabbing incident, leading police to deploy water cannons to disperse demonstrators. The unrest highlights ongoing tensions in the area, with community members expressing anger and frustration over the violence. Authorities are monitoring the situation closely as they work to restore order.

clusters.title: Knicks Make Historic Comeback to Lead NBA Finals 3-1
clusters.summary: The New York Knicks achieved a remarkable comeback, overcoming a 29-point deficit to defeat the San Antonio Spurs 107-106 in Game 4 of the NBA Finals. This victory marks the largest comeback in NBA Finals history, giving the Knicks a 3-1 series lead. The win highlights the team's resilience and positions them one game away from clinching the championship.

items.title: Knicks complete record rally from 29 points down and beat Spurs 107-106 for 3-1 NBA Finals lead - AP News
items.description: Knicks complete record rally from 29 points down and beat Spurs 107-106 for 3-1 NBA Finals lead  AP News

clusters.title: Southern Baptists Move to Ban Women from Pastoral Roles
clusters.summary: The Southern Baptist Convention has voted to advance a formal ban on women serving as pastors in member churches. This decision reflects ongoing debates within the denomination regarding gender roles and leadership. The move is significant as it may impact the future of women's participation in church leadership across the Southern Baptist community.
